topics.title: Present Continuous
topics.explanation: We use "am/is/are + verb-ing" for things happening right now.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "am/is/are + فعل-ing" للأشياء التي تحدث الآن.

examples.en: I am reading a book right now.
She is cooking dinner.
They are playing in the garden.
We aren't watching TV at the moment.

topics.title: Going to (Future Plans)
topics.explanation: We use "going to" for plans we already decided on before now.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "going to" للخطط التي قررناها بالفعل قبل الآن.

examples.en: I am going to visit my grandma tomorrow.
We are going to have a party this weekend.
She is going to study medicine.
Are you going to join the football team?
examples.ar: هل ستنضم إلى فريق كرة القدم؟

topics.title: Present Continuous for Future Plans
topics.explanation: We can also use present continuous for planned future arrangements, especially with a time expression.
topics.explanationAr: يمكننا أيضًا استخدام المضارع المستمر للترتيبات المستقبلية المخطط لها.

examples.en: I am meeting my friend at five o'clock.
We are flying to Dubai next Monday.
examples.ar: سنسافر إلى دبي يوم الاثنين القادم.

examples.en: She is starting her new school in September.
Are you coming to the party tonight?

topics.title: Will (Future Predictions)
topics.explanation: We use "will" to make predictions or decisions about the future made at the moment of speaking.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "will" للتنبؤ بالمستقبل أو لقرارات تُتخذ في لحظة الحديث.

examples.en: I think it will rain tomorrow.
She will be a great doctor one day.
I'll help you with your bags.
They won't be at school tomorrow.

topics.title: Comparatives & Superlatives with Long Adjectives
topics.explanation: Longer adjectives (three or more syllables) use "more" for comparatives and "most" for superlatives, instead of "-er/-est".
topics.explanationAr: الصفات الطويلة تستخدم "more" للمقارنة و"most" للتفضيل بدلاً من "-er/-est".

examples.en: This movie is more exciting than the last one.
examples.ar: هذا الفيلم أكثر إثارة من السابق.

examples.en: She is the most intelligent student in class.
This question is more difficult than that one.
It was the most beautiful sunset I've seen.

topics.title: Should / Shouldn't
topics.explanation: We use "should" to give advice or say what is a good idea, and "shouldn't" for what is not a good idea.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "should" لإعطاء نصيحة، و"shouldn't" لما ليس فكرة جيدة.

examples.en: You should drink more water.
examples.ar: يجب أن تشرب المزيد من الماء.

examples.en: She should study for her exam.
You shouldn't eat too much sugar.
examples.ar: لا يجب أن تأكل الكثير من السكر.

examples.en: We should leave now to catch the bus.
examples.ar: يجب أن نغادر الآن للحاق بالحافلة.

topics.title: Have to / Don't have to
topics.titleAr: have to و don't have to
topics.explanation: "Have to" means something is necessary. "Don't have to" means it isn't necessary, but you can if you want.
topics.explanationAr: "have to" تعني أن الأمر ضروري. "don't have to" تعني أنه غير ضروري.

examples.en: I have to finish my homework tonight.
She has to wake up early for school.
You don't have to come if you're busy.
examples.ar: لست مضطرًا للحضور إذا كنت مشغولاً.

examples.en: We don't have to wear uniforms on Fridays.
examples.ar: لسنا مضطرين لارتداء الزي المدرسي يوم الجمعة.

topics.title: Adverbs of Manner
topics.explanation: Adverbs of manner describe how an action is done. Many are formed by adding "-ly" to an adjective: quick → quickly.
topics.explanationAr: ظروف الحال تصف كيفية القيام بالفعل. تتكون الكثير منها بإضافة "-ly" إلى الصفة.

examples.en: She ran quickly to catch the bus.
He speaks English fluently.
Please drive carefully in the rain.
examples.ar: من فضلك قد بحذر في المطر.

examples.en: The teacher explained the lesson clearly.
